purpose: action_ref as shared audit handle for MCP tool calls. Demonstrates independent operator-agnostic verification: any party holding the four preimage fields can recompute the handle without trusting the emitting system or calling signed_or_introspected. The preimage fields map directly to the bind_receipt_to tuple (agent_id, action_type, scope, timestamp). Vector 3 is a negative example: hashed scope breaks independent recomputability — the verifier cannot recover the pre-hash value without the commitment record.

vectors.description: MCP tool call — agent requests filesystem read under session scope. action_ref is the shared audit handle: client, server, and any third-party auditor can recompute it from the four preimage fields alone, without querying the MCP server or verifying a signature.

bind_receipt_to_mapping.agent_id: executing agent at issuance time — terminal executor after full delegation resolution
bind_receipt_to_mapping.action_type: MCP method or tool name as semantic label
bind_receipt_to_mapping.scope: requested-intent scope — what the agent requested at the point of the call
bind_receipt_to_mapping.timestamp: RFC 3339 UTC with 3-digit ms precision — issuance time, not server-admission time

vectors.description: MCP tool call with delegation — orchestrator delegates to subagent, subagent executes. agent_id is the terminal executor (subagent-executor), not the orchestrator. The orchestrator's identity belongs in delegation_ref, not in the action_ref preimage. This is the key distinction from signed_or_introspected: the audit handle is anchored to who executed, independently verifiable.

bind_receipt_to_mapping.agent_id: subagent-executor — terminal executor, not the orchestrator that delegated
bind_receipt_to_mapping.scope: mcp:github:write — requested-intent at point of action

vectors.description: NEGATIVE EXAMPLE — scope is SHA-256 of the intent document instead of the intent label. This breaks the primary property: a verifier holding the four preimage fields cannot recompute action_ref without also retrieving the commitment record to recover the pre-hash value. The resulting action_ref is technically valid (bytes hash correctly) but is not a shared audit handle — it is an operator-bound receipt. Compare with mcp-01: same agent, same action_type, same timestamp, different scope encoding produces a different action_ref that an external auditor cannot independently derive.
vectors.anti_pattern: scope = SHA-256(intent_document) instead of the intent label string
vectors.consequence: verifier must retrieve commitment record to recompute — breaks operator-independent auditability
vectors.note: action_ref differs from mcp-01 despite identical agent_id, action_type, and timestamp. An auditor given only the four preimage fields from the commitment record computes 9636410f... but cannot verify it matches the original intent without the pre-hash value.
